title: GNC sensor suite upgrade: IMU P-1092→P-1093, GPS antenna P-1097→P-1098
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-228.

Raised by: Sam Dreher (Nav/GNC Software Lead), 2024-12-10.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Felix Herrera (Systems Integration), 2024-12-18.

Summary: Replace the GNC sensor suite — IMU module from ICM-42688-P (P-1092) to ADIS16505-2 (P-1093), and GPS antenna from L1/L2 patch (P-1097) to L1/L2/L5 helical (P-1098).

Rationale: Flight test campaign phase 1 (DOC-467) revealed two GNC performance deficiencies:

1. IMU vibration error (KES-089, KES-091): The ICM-42688-P (P-1092) MEMS resonant frequency (4.8kHz) is excited by motor vibration harmonics from P-1041 motors at mid-throttle, causing attitude estimation errors up to 2.1° roll. The DOC-455 (GNC ICD) requirement is 0.5° attitude accuracy. The ADIS16505-2 (P-1093) has a 18kHz resonant frequency well above the motor harmonic range, and its vibration rectification error is 8x better (0.006 vs 0.05°/s/g²).

2. GPS dropout during manoeuvres: The patch antenna (P-1097) loses reliable sky view at roll angles above 15°, causing GPS dropouts. The helical antenna (P-1098) provides near-hemispherical coverage and maintains >20dBHz at 30° elevation. It also adds L5 support, improving multi-path rejection in complex sea-state environments.

Both changes agreed at WIKI-067 (GNC Sensor Suite Design Review, 2024-12-03).

Impact:
- P-1092: Obsoleted, replaced by P-1093
- P-1097: Obsoleted, replaced by P-1098
- SRC-007 (nav_filter.c): EKF IMU driver update for ADIS SPI interface — Dev Patel
- SRC-009 (gps_driver.c): GPS driver update for L5 band — Dev Patel
- DOC-455: GNC ICD to Rev B (attitude accuracy requirement now achievable)
- DOC-456: GPS Performance Specification to Rev B
- DOC-467: Flight Test Report to be updated after re-qualification flight tests
